

# About Me

I am a doctoral student in the Developmental Psychology program at the University of Chicago and an Institute of Education Sciences (IES) Fellow.


<center>

![](Carr_Dania.jpg){width=400px}

</center>



I am a doctoral student in the Developmental Psychology program at the University of Chicago and an Institute of Education Sciences (IES) Fellow.


<center>

![](Carr_Dania.jpg){width=400px}

</center>


<br>


### Professional Interests

 My research examines how socio-emotional, cognitive, and linguistic factors incluence children's mathematical thinking. I am interested in applying my research, data analysis, and data visualization skills to uncover patterns in educational data, generate actionable insights, and support evidence-based strategy and decision making.


<br>



### Personal Interests 

I love playing pool, relaxing at the Chicago lakefront (especially during summer months), and hiking. I also love spending time with my husband and my dog. 

[will insert picture of husband and dog]
